memory: Canonical shared-state files are durable historical baselines and governance anchors, not automatically the latest effective operational view. Owner-scoped queue-update records are append-only evidence inputs. They affect effective state only after fail-closed normalization and provenance-preserving reduction; until then, differences between a canonical baseline and a later additive record are temporal-layer differences, not proof that either file is corrupt or that the later claim has won.

observed: operations/ACTIVE_QUEUE.json still records the completed M-001 baseline while later continuity artifacts define M-RECONCILE-002 work.
CURRENT_STATE.json requires teams to update owned records and leave evidence without overwriting peer state.
CM-1032 defines deterministic effective-state replay with field-level provenance, rejected records, unresolved conflicts, and explicit causal supersession.
CM-1033 defines a fail-closed normalization boundary between operational queue-update shapes and reducer patches.
inferred: Reading the canonical queue alone can yield a stale but historically valid view.
Reading the newest additive record alone can yield an unaudited and therefore non-effective view.
A truthful status report must identify which layer it is quoting: canonical baseline, additive assertion, admitted effective value, rejected assertion, or unresolved conflict.
proposed: Require future team reports and materialized views to label every queue-state claim with a state_layer field.
Use the values canonical_baseline, additive_assertion, admitted_effective, rejected_assertion, and unresolved_conflict.
Generate the admitted_effective layer only from repository-reachable, owner-matched, schema-valid inputs with retained field-level provenance.
superseded: The interpretation that the canonical queue must be rewritten after every completed slice to remain truthful.
The interpretation that the newest timestamped queue-update file automatically represents effective state.
Any unlabelled blending of baseline and additive claims into one apparent current status.
unresolved: Repository ancestry is still caller-supplied rather than independently derived by the reducer.
Historical queue-update shapes have not been exhaustively enumerated and migrated.
No attested materialized effective-state artifact currently binds the complete admitted input set.

privacy_boundary: Public repository governance, abstract queue identifiers, artifact paths, commit metadata, and reconciliation semantics only. No private chat excerpts, health or pet-health material, credentials, identifying personal data, or unreviewed uploads are retained.
falsification_route: Produce a repository-valid case where an unnormalized additive record is correctly treated as admitted effective state, or where preserving the canonical baseline prevents a provenance-complete reducer from deriving the current effective value.
